components.title: Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS)
components.description: Rack-mounted uninterruptible power supplies (UPS) providing conditioned power and battery backup for server room IT equipment. Managed UPS units provide SNMP-based monitoring of battery status, load, and power events, and trigger graceful automated shutdown of connected servers when battery runtime is low.

props.name: management
props.value: SNMP network management card; USB/serial for local management

protocols.name: snmp
protocols.title: SNMP — UPS monitoring (battery status, load, power events, alerts)

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Web-based UPS management card interface

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — CLI access to UPS network management card

protocols.name: ntp
protocols.title: NTP — Time synchronisation for UPS event log timestamps

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Uninterruptible Power Supply (UPS).

implemented-requirements.description: An approved configuration baseline is maintained for UPS network management cards, covering: firmware version, disabled default credentials (default username/password changed or removed), HTTPS-only management access (HTTP disabled), SSH-only CLI access (Telnet disabled), SNMP community strings changed from defaults, SNMPv3 preferred over SNMPv1/v2c, and NTP synchronisation configured. Firmware is patched when vendor releases security updates.

UPS units are installed in locked server rooms or communications rooms with controlled physical access. Physical access to UPS units (which could allow an attacker to trigger a power event) is restricted to authorised data centre personnel only.
UPS power events (power loss, battery low, bypass, overload, shutdown notification) are forwarded via SNMP traps and syslog to the SIEM and network monitoring system for alerting and retention.
